fields.why: Illusion and sensory spells need runtime data for what their manifestations can and cannot create, especially when the prose separates sound, image, smell, light, and other sensory limits by mode.
fields.comment: Stores spell-created sensory manifestations and their option-specific limits. Purpose: effects[].sensoryManifestation exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about sensory mechanics hidden inside illusion descriptions. Populate it from structured markdown labels matching `Sensory Manifestation ...` after the canonical prose has been reviewed. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sensoryManifestation`. The value type is `object`. Omit only when the spell does not create a mechanically constrained sensory manifestation; otherwise use explicit `not_applicable` values inside the object for option fields that do not apply.

log.why: Minor Illusion's Sound and Image modes have different allowed senses, excluded senses, sound timing, and image size limits that were previously flattened into prose.

fields.why: Illusion reveal mechanics need a runtime home separate from escape checks because discerning an illusion can change only one creature's perception without ending the spell.
fields.comment: Stores how an illusion can be examined or physically revealed and what happens for creatures that discern it. Purpose: effects[].illusion exists so Study checks, physical-interaction reveal rules, and post-reveal states are not mistaken for ordinary escape checks or flavor. Populate it from structured markdown labels matching `Illusion ...` after the canonical prose has been reviewed. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion`. The value type is `object`. Omit only when the spell has no mechanically relevant illusion reveal/discernment rule; otherwise use explicit `not_applicable` values inside the object for parts that do not apply.

log.why: Minor Illusion lets creatures Study the sound or image, reveals image mode through physical interaction, and makes the illusion faint only to discerning creatures.
